title: Running from the call
summary_text: The LORD sends Jonah to Nineveh, but he flees by ship in the opposite direction. A storm, the sailors' fear, and Jonah's casting overboard reveal that the sea obeys God. Running from God only deepens the crisis.
key_points: The LORD sends Jonah to Nineveh, but he flees by ship in the opposite direction.

Running from God only deepens the crisis.

title: Prayer from the fish
summary_text: Jonah prays from the belly of the fish after being swallowed in mercy. He remembers the LORD from the depths and vows thanksgiving. The fish spits him out, showing that deliverance begins with repentance.
key_points: Jonah prays from the belly of the fish after being swallowed in mercy.

title: Nineveh repents
summary_text: Jonah goes to Nineveh, preaches judgment, and the city believes God. From king to cattle, the people fast and turn from violence, and God relents from the threatened disaster. The chapter is a stunning picture of Gentile repentance.
key_points: Jonah goes to Nineveh, preaches judgment, and the city believes God.
From king to cattle, the people fast and turn from violence, and God relents from the threatened disaster.
The chapter is a stunning picture of Gentile repentance.

title: The plant and the prophet
summary_text: Jonah is angry that God spares the city he wanted judged. The LORD uses a plant, a worm, and the scorching wind to expose Jonah's narrow compassion. The book ends by asking whether God's mercy should extend to more than one people.
key_points: Jonah is angry that God spares the city he wanted judged.
